metadata.scheme_name: One Time Financial Support for Economically Weaker Meritorious ST Students

raw_data.details: The Tribal Welfare Department, Government of Tripura was launched by the "One Time Financial Support for Economically Weaker Meritorious ST Students" scheme aimed at providing financial assistance to economically weaker meritorious Scheduled Tribe (ST) students. This support enables them to pursue professional and other courses in government-recognized institutes and colleges with the necessary financial support to continue their education without financial constraints.
raw_data.benefits: Financial Support: A one-time financial support of ₹1,00,000 will be provided to selected students in two installments:1st Installment: ₹50,000 released immediately after selection. 2nd Installment: ₹50,000 upon production of pass certificates for the 1st year course.
raw_data.eligibility: The student should be a domicile of Tripura. The student should belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) community. The student should have passed Class 10th (Madhyamik) for pre-12th courses or Class 12th (H. S level) for post-12th courses in 2022 with at least 45% marks. The student must have taken admission in any professional course listed in [Annexure-A and Annexure-B](https://twd.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/Notification_for_Public_Domain-OTFS.pdf) during the academic year 2022-23, with a course duration of at least 2 years.
raw_data.exclusions: The scheme will not prevent eligible candidates from receiving the Post Matric Scholarship as per the norms. Courses listed in [Annexure-C](https://twd.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/Notification_for_Public_Domain-OTFS.pdf) are not recommended under the OTFS scheme.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineNSP Registration Process for Fresh Application:
Step 01: The students applying for the scholarship for the first time need to "Register" using the icon “New Registration” at the NSP Portal URL - [https://scholarships.gov.in/](https://scholarships.gov.in/), by providing accurate and correct information as per their documents.
Step 02: After successfully getting the registration done, students will get their “Student Registration ID”.
Step 03: Through “Student Registration ID” students will be able to login into the account using the icon “Fresh Application” at NSP Portal. Application Process for "One-Time Financial Support":All eligible students will receive an auto-generated message from the portal notifying them of their eligibility. Go to [https://bms.tripura.gov.in](https://bms.tripura.gov.in/bms/index.jsp? NICBMSSecurity=66M5-CY12-7G0E-I60I-4VPI-W8WY-5AKG-269V) and select "Citizen Login."If you haven’t registered yet, click on "Register here" and fill in your Name, Email ID, and Mobile number. After receiving the OTP on your mobile number or email ID, enter it to complete the verification. Once the profile page is open, fill in the ration card number to fetch your details. You will receive a message confirming that your profile is complete. Click on "Apply Online" and select the scheme titled "One-Time Financial Support for Economically Weaker Meritorious ST Students" EnrollSelect "Proceed" to open the application form. Fill in your bank details and upload the required documents. Enter your NSP Registration ID and Academic details, then click "Next."Select "Final Submit" to complete your application. An Application ID will be generated. Click on "View and Print Acknowledgement Slip" for your records.
raw_data.documents_required: Passport-size Photograph. Scheduled Tribe (ST) Certificate. Income Certificate (issued by Sub Divisional Magistrate (SDM)/DCM). Master Degree Marksheet. Bachelor Degree Marksheet. Class 10th Marksheet. Class 12th Marksheet. Diploma Marksheet. Bonafide Certificate from the Institute.

This support enables them to pursue professional and other courses in government-recognized institutes and colleges with the necessary financial support to continue their education without financial constraints. BenefitsFinancial Support: A one-time financial support of ₹1,00,000 will be provided to selected students in two installments:1st Installment: ₹50,000 released immediately after selection. 2nd Installment: ₹50,000 upon production of pass certificates for the 1st year course. EligibilityThe student should be a domicile of Tripura. The student should belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) community. The student should have passed Class 10th (Madhyamik) for pre-12th courses or Class 12th (H. S level) for post-12th courses in 2022 with at least 45% marks. The student must have taken admission in any professional course listed in [Annexure-A and Annexure-B](https://twd.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/Notification_for_Public_Domain-OTFS.pdf) during the academic year 2022-23, with a course duration of at least 2 years. ExclusionsThe scheme will not prevent eligible candidates from receiving the Post Matric Scholarship as per the norms. Courses listed in [Annexure-C](https://twd.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/Notification_for_Public_Domain-OTFS.pdf) are not recommended under the OTFS scheme. Application ProcessOnlineNSP Registration Process for Fresh Application:Step 01: The students applying for the scholarship for the first time need to "Register" using the icon “New Registration” at the NSP Portal URL - [https://scholarships.gov.in/](https://scholarships.gov.in/), by providing accurate and correct information as per their documents. Step 02: After successfully getting the registration done, students will get their “Student Registration ID”. Step 03: Through “Student Registration ID” students will be able to login into the account using the icon “Fresh Application” at NSP Portal. Application Process for "One-Time Financial Support":All eligible students will receive an auto-generated message from the portal notifying them of their eligibility. NICBMSSecurity=66M5-CY12-7G0E-I60I-4VPI-W8WY-5AKG-269V) and select "Citizen Login."If you haven’t registered yet, click on "Register here" and fill in your Name, Email ID, and Mobile number. After receiving the OTP on your mobile number or email ID, enter it to complete the verification. Once the profile page is open, fill in the ration card number to fetch your details. You will receive a message confirming that your profile is complete. Click on "Apply Online" and select the scheme titled "One-Time Financial Support for Economically Weaker Meritorious ST Students" EnrollSelect "Proceed" to open the application form. Income Certificate (issued by Sub Divisional Magistrate (SDM)/DCM). Master Degree Marksheet. Bachelor Degree Marksheet. Class 10th Marksheet. Class 12th Marksheet. Diploma Marksheet. Bonafide Certificate from the Institute. Frequently Asked QuestionsWho is eligible for the OTFS scheme? ST students who are domiciled in Tripura, passed Class X or XII with at least 45% marks, and are fresh applicants in the NSP portal for the 2022-23 session are eligible.

How much financial support is provided under the OTFS scheme? A total of ₹1,00,000 is provided in two installments of ₹50,000 each. Is there a specific course list for which the OTFS scheme is applicable? Yes, the scheme is applicable for courses listed in Annexure-A and Annexure-B. How many seats are available for the OTFS scheme? The target number of seats for the OTFS scheme for the year is 500, but this number is subject to variation based on the availability of funds. Can I apply if I am in the second year of my course? No, only first-year/first-semester students are eligible. How are the seats distributed under the OTFS scheme? Seats are distributed between post-12th and pre-12th courses in the ratio of 84:16. Is the scheme available for students in private colleges? The scheme is only available for students in government-recognized institutes/colleges. What is the selection process for the OTFS scheme? Selection is based on merit, considering the percentage of marks and other eligibility criteria. Can I apply offline for the OTFS scheme? No, applications are only accepted online through the portal. What if my NSP data is incorrect? You should verify and update your NSP data before applying for the OTFS scheme. Is there a specific format for marks calculation? Yes, the calculation of percentage is based on the best of 5 subjects or the CGPA system, proportionally adjusted. Can the scheme be amended or withdrawn? Yes, the Tribal Welfare Department reserves the right to amend or withdraw the scheme without prior notice. What documents are required for the application? Ration Card number, NSP ID, Class X/XII marksheets, bank details, and Aadhaar card are required. Sources And References[Guidelines](https://twd.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/Notification_for_Public_Domain-OTFS.pdf)OkWas this helpful?
raw_data.scheme_name: One Time Financial Support for Economically Weaker Meritorious ST Students

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the OTFS scheme?
faqs.answer: ST students who are domiciled in Tripura, passed Class X or XII with at least 45% marks, and are fresh applicants in the NSP portal for the 2022-23 session are eligible.

faqs.question: How much financial support is provided under the OTFS scheme?
faqs.answer: A total of ₹1,00,000 is provided in two installments of ₹50,000 each.

faqs.question: Is there a specific course list for which the OTFS scheme is applicable?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme is applicable for courses listed in Annexure-A and Annexure-B.

faqs.question: How many seats are available for the OTFS scheme?
faqs.answer: The target number of seats for the OTFS scheme for the year is 500, but this number is subject to variation based on the availability of funds.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I am in the second year of my course?
faqs.answer: No, only first-year/first-semester students are eligible.

faqs.question: How are the seats distributed under the OTFS scheme?
faqs.answer: Seats are distributed between post-12th and pre-12th courses in the ratio of 84:16.

faqs.question: Is the scheme available for students in private colleges?
faqs.answer: The scheme is only available for students in government-recognized institutes/colleges.

faqs.question: What is the selection process for the OTFS scheme?
faqs.answer: Selection is based on merit, considering the percentage of marks and other eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: Can I apply offline for the OTFS scheme?
faqs.answer: No, applications are only accepted online through the portal.

faqs.question: What if my NSP data is incorrect?
faqs.answer: You should verify and update your NSP data before applying for the OTFS scheme.

faqs.question: Is there a specific format for marks calculation?
faqs.answer: Yes, the calculation of percentage is based on the best of 5 subjects or the CGPA system, proportionally adjusted.

faqs.question: Can the scheme be amended or withdrawn?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Tribal Welfare Department reserves the right to amend or withdraw the scheme without prior notice.

faqs.question: What documents are required for the application?
faqs.answer: Ration Card number, NSP ID, Class X/XII marksheets, bank details, and Aadhaar card are required.
